problem_statement: AI-powered development platforms accelerate creation of web applications by developers lacking security expertise, but security assessment cannot keep pace, and pattern-based tools miss contextually exploitable and business-logic vulnerabilities. The paper presents an autonomous multi-agent system to scale web application security assessment with validated exploits.
magnitude_evidence: "studies showing that up to 40% of AI-generated code contains vulnerabilities [21]"; "GitHub Copilot generates code containing vulnerabilities in 40% of CWE-targeted scenarios [21]"
insufficiency_evidence: "a study of seven Java SAST tools found only 12.7% of real-world vulnerabilities were detected, with the union of all tools missing 71% [16]"; scanners miss business logic vulnerabilities and produce high false positives; manual review "remains constrained by manual processes"
